mnemonics: Pandas Strings Need Special Syntax: Use .str to access string methods in a vectorized way.
keyPoints: Vectorized operations apply a function to every element in a Series/array at once, without an explicit loop.
Pandas provides a `.str` accessor to apply common string methods (like .upper(), .contains()) to every element in a Series.
These operations are essential for efficiently cleaning and transforming string-based data columns.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use the .str accessor and trying to call a string method directly on a Series (e.g., `df['col'].upper()` instead of `df['col'].str.upper()`).
Assuming vectorized string operations work on a DataFrame with missing/NaN values without first handling or filling those nulls, which can cause errors.
